fields.summary: Improve yarn logs to fetch container logs without specifying nodeId

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description: Currently, for the finished application, we can get the container logs without specify node id, but we need to enable yarn.timeline-service.generic-application-history.enabled.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

comments.body: what we could improve is if yarn.timeline-service.generic-application-history.enabled is disabled, for the finished application, we could read through all the log files in HDFS and try to find the specific container log.

This message was automatically generated.

Thanks for the patch [~xgong]! I think the patch itself is ok - just a minor change - change getAppState to appStateKnown.

However, it looks we need a re-factoring of the code in LogsCLI; the run function in particular is becoming too big and too unmanageable. What do you think? Should we file another JIRA about cleaning up the code?

Thanks , varun for the review.
Attached a new patch to address your comment.

Thanks, junping for the review.

bq. Why we must need node address if app is not finished? Can we ask RM to get the nodeAddress instead?

So, currently, we could only get the running container report from RM, and get the finished container report from ATS. It would go to this else-if clause when:
1. the application is running
2. the container is finished
3. this container is not the AM Container, and we only send AM Container metrics to ATS.

This is a good catch. This makes people confuse.
{code}
+          System.out.println("Please specify the nodeAddress, and use "
+              + "yarn logs -applicationId <appId> -containerId <containerId> "
+              + "--nodeAddress <nodeAddress> to get the container logs");
{code}
Have modified the print out message, and make it more clear to users.

[~vvasudev] [~junping_du]
Have created https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-4993 to track the refactory work.

Thanks for the patch [~xgong]! I think the patch itself is ok - just a minor change - change getAppState to appStateKnown.

However, it looks we need a re-factoring of the code in LogsCLI; the run function in particular is becoming too big and too unmanageable. What do you think? Should we file another JIRA about cleaning up the code?

Thanks , varun for the review.
Attached a new patch to address your comment.

Thanks, junping for the review.

bq. Why we must need node address if app is not finished? Can we ask RM to get the nodeAddress instead?

So, currently, we could only get the running container report from RM, and get the finished container report from ATS. It would go to this else-if clause when:
1. the application is running
2. the container is finished
3. this container is not the AM Container, and we only send AM Container metrics to ATS.

This is a good catch. This makes people confuse.
{code}
+          System.out.println("Please specify the nodeAddress, and use "
+              + "yarn logs -applicationId <appId> -containerId <containerId> "
+              + "--nodeAddress <nodeAddress> to get the container logs");
{code}
Have modified the print out message, and make it more clear to users.

[~vvasudev] [~junping_du]
Have created https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-4993 to track the refactory work.
